alignments.candidate_evidence: 8 years at Google Devices & Services with two promotions, scaling global commercial operations and transformation programs; experience in requirements definition, logic design, UAT, rollout, and transformation—demonstrating ability to bridge product strategy and cross-functional execution
alignments.job_requirement: Partner with Product Managers and Engineers to scope, build, and ship AI agents; play a central role in agent development, combining product strategy, conversational design, and customer insight

alignments.candidate_evidence: Led transformation programs at Google with responsibility for ownership, stakeholder management, and execution across multiple workstreams; proven ability to coordinate complex projects
alignments.job_requirement: Drive execution and delivery of multiple complex, high-visibility agent development projects; manage stakeholders through the full development lifecycle

alignments.candidate_evidence: Positioned as Strategy and Operations leader managing global commercial operations; must communicate across technical and non-technical teams in transformation contexts
alignments.job_requirement: Outstanding communication skills; convey complex technical information to non-technical stakeholders

alignments.candidate_evidence: Transformation and program leadership background typically requires strong analytical and problem-solving capability in ambiguous, cross-functional environments
alignments.job_requirement: Problem-solving and analytical skills to grasp complex concepts, identify risks, develop mitigation strategies

alignments.candidate_evidence: Strategy and Operations discipline implies data-informed decision-making; commercial operations scaling suggests quantitative rigor
alignments.job_requirement: Contribute data-driven, strategic insights to customers and internal team decisions

alignments.candidate_evidence: Product Operations and GTM strategy background suggests customer-facing advisory experience, though AI-specific strategy depth is unclear
alignments.job_requirement: Be a trusted advisor to customers and drive their AI strategies

gaps.gap: No explicit AI agent or conversational AI design experience mentioned; candidate background is Google Devices & Services commercial operations, not AI product or agent development
gaps.mitigation: Strong program leadership and cross-functional product strategy experience translates well to agent development contexts. On-the-job learning in conversational design and agent-specific best practices is feasible given hands-on role scope and Sierra's mentorship culture.

gaps.gap: Limited consulting or classic case-team experience; candidate profile emphasizes internal product operations and transformation rather than external advisory or fractional advisory roles
gaps.mitigation: Role is internal-facing with customer partnership, not pure consulting. Transformation program leadership provides relevant stakeholder management and advisory framing skills; customer-facing advisory can be developed through onboarding.

gaps.gap: Product experience derived from requirements, design, UAT, and rollout rather than native PM/SDLC depth or product management frameworks; unclear exposure to user research, feature prioritization, or roadmap ownership
gaps.mitigation: Agent Strategist role emphasizes execution, design, and delivery coordination over PM methodology depth. Candidate's transformation and operational excellence background is well-suited; gaps in PM-specific practices are secondary.

gaps.gap: Technical depth in software development and engineering principles not explicitly documented; candidate has basic Python and is actively learning, but not a strong technical foundation
gaps.mitigation: JD specifies 'strong understanding' of software development and engineering principles as 'technical expertise' (not a must-have). Candidate's program leadership and cross-functional coordination experience demonstrate ability to work effectively with engineers; hands-on exposure in the role will accelerate technical fluency.

output.summary: Google Strategy & Operations leader (8 years, 2 promotions) with strong commercial transformation, program execution, and cross-functional stakeholder management experience. Role family fit is solid for Agent Strategist—combining product strategy, execution, and customer partnership—but conversational AI and agent-specific expertise are absent. Gaps are learnable given hands-on scope and mentorship; technical depth is supplementary to the execution, design, and advisory focus of the role.
uncertainties: Depth of customer advisory experience and familiarity with external advisory or forward-deployed strategy models unclear from profile
Specific technical depth in software development and engineering principles not detailed; only basic Python mentioned
Conversational design and AI agent domain knowledge entirely absent—unclear if candidate has any adjacent experience (e.g., NLP, chatbots, voice products)
MBA or equivalent technology + operations credential not mentioned; unclear if candidate meets the 'strong plus' qualification
German language fluency stated as 'native,' but unclear if candidate meets London-based role location expectation or visa/mobility situation
